# A Layered Grammar of Graphics: ggplot2 methods

Getting Started

Use [p_load()] function to install the collection of R packages under tidyverse.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse)
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```
:::


## Histogram of Maths Scores

::: panel-tabset
## R Graphics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
hist(exam_data$MATHS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex01_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## ggplot2


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 10, color = "black", fill = "lightgrey") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of exam_data$MATHS",
       x = "exam_data$MATHS",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_bw()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex01_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

:::


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS, fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "grey30")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex01_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::
